title: Coolant and a method to control the ph and resistivity of coolant used in a heat exchanger
abstract: Implementations described herein generally relate to substrate processing equipment and more particularly to methods and compositions for temperature control of substrate processing equipment. In one implementation, a method of cooling a processing chamber component is provided. The method comprises introducing an inert purge gas into a supply reservoir containing a coolant and flowing the treated coolant to a processing chamber component to cool the processing chamber component. The coolant initially comprises deionized water and a water-soluble base.
claims: Claims Claims: 1 . A method of cooling a processing chamber component, comprising: introducing an inert purge gas into a supply reservoir containing a coolant to form a treated coolant, wherein the coolant initially comprises: deionized water; and a water-soluble base; and flowing the treated coolant to a processing chamber component to cool the processing chamber component. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the water-soluble base is sodium hydroxide. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein the water-soluble base is nitrogen-free. 4. The method of claim 1 , wherein the water-soluble base is ammonia-free. 5. The method of claim 1 , wherein the treated coolant is nitrogen-free. 6. The method of claim 1 , wherein the purge gas is nitrogen gas (N 2 ). 7. The method of claim 1 , wherein the purge gas is flowed into the supply reservoir at a flow rate greater than or equal to 0.8 Liters/minute. 8. The method of claim 1 , wherein the purge gas is flowed into the supply reservoir at a flow rate between 0.8 Liters/minute and 20 Liters/minute. 9. The method of claim 1 , wherein the coolant has a pH between 8.0 and 12.5. 10. The method of claim 1 , wherein the purge gas is delivered to the space above the liquid coolant in the supply reservoir. 1 1 . The method of claim 10, wherein the purge gas removes CO2 from the space above the liquid coolant in the supply reservoir. 12. The method of claim 1 1 , wherein the purge gas removes dissolved C0 2 from the liquid coolant in the supply reservoir. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein flowing the treated coolant to the processing chamber component to cool the processing chamber component includes flowing the treated coolant through a channel having an exposed metallic surface. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the exposed metallic surface is a copper surface. 15. The method of claim 1 , wherein the processing chamber component is the energy source of a laser annealing system.
